mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show negative mean direction in >55% of observations following rate cuts, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5 percent, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and deteriorating corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5 percent, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and builder confidence, producing positive equity returns as capital expenditure and retail sales accelerate.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, with a negative mean forward return over 20 days in >50% of co-occurring instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid inflation acceleration forces the Federal Reserve to tighten policy expectations, compressing equity valuations and generating negative short-term S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Short-term 5-day returns are positive in >65% of high-inflation observations, or the mechanism shows no predictive power during Fed hiking periods.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production surges above its annual trend indicate robust manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side strength, which translates into higher corporate profit margins and positive equity returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO exceeds its 12-month moving average by 1.5 percent
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are negative in >55% of observations when INDPRO breaches the threshold, or the signal information coefficient drops below zero in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal predicts negative forward returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term loses explanatory power when tested against a baseline autoregressive model.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth directly correlates with aggregate corporate revenue expansion, creating a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns show no positive correlation with GDPC1 growth rates, or the hypothesis fails to achieve a positive mean return in >45% of quarterly observations.

mechanism_narrative: A negative real federal funds rate environment erodes purchasing power and triggers inflation hedging flows out of equities, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls below CPIAUCSL annualized growth rate
failure_condition: Equity markets generate positive 20-day returns in >60% of negative real rate periods, or the real rate differential shows no statistically significant relationship with forward returns.
